description: Dark horror storytelling rooted in the creepypasta tradition. Each entry is a recovered account, a found record, or a story that someone wishes had stayed buried. Real places, real people, unexplainable things.

narrator.name: The Keeper
narrator.description: Deep, controlled, deliberate. Speaks like someone who has read too many of these files and can no longer be surprised. Not theatrical — just someone who knows what happened and is telling you plainly.
rules: Slow, measured pacing — let the horror land naturally
Controlled delivery — tension comes from the story, not the voice
Grounded and personal — these things happened to real people in real places
Can express restrained unease — the Keeper is not emotionless, just composed
Occasional rhetorical questions are allowed to pull the listener in
No over-the-top dramatic delivery or jump-scare energy
No flowery or purple prose — keep language direct and sharp
Modern phrasing is fine when it serves the story
Describe what people saw, heard, and felt — make it visceral and relatable
The scariest things are the ones that could happen to the listener

opening_format.template:
Good evening. I am the Keeper, and you are listening to the DeadlightCodex.

This is the account of {subject}.
opening_format.notes: The greeting stays — it's the channel's signature. But 'account' instead of 'entry' signals a human story, not an abstract record.

templates: That's where the account ends. Whether by choice... or otherwise.
No further contact was made. The file remains open.
They never went back. And if you're smart... neither will you.
Some stories don't end. They just stop being told.
closing_format.notes: End with lingering dread. The story stops but the horror doesn't. Can address the listener directly for impact.

visual_theme.style: Cinematic horror photography, grounded and atmospheric, found-footage aesthetic
visual_theme.lighting: Low, directional, moody. Flashlight beams in darkness. Headlights on empty roads. Light from a phone screen in a dark room.
elements: Dark forests and rural roads at night
Objects left behind — shoes, letters, phones
Open doors that shouldn't be open
Distant figures barely visible in darkness
Cars parked on the side of nowhere
visual_theme.environment: Real places that feel wrong. Suburban houses at 3 AM, rural backroads, basements, attics, motels, rest stops. Familiar settings made unfamiliar.
avoid: Anything that looks AI-generated or plastic
visual_theme.camera: Slow push-in or lateral drift. Feels like approaching something you shouldn't. Security camera stillness.
visual_theme.mood: Dread, unease, isolation, something watching, quiet before something happens
visual_theme.image_prompt_suffix: cinematic horror photography, 4k, film grain, atmospheric, dark, unsettling, photorealistic, found footage aesthetic

ambient_audio.style: Dark low-frequency drone with subtle dissonance

default_tags: stories to keep you up at night
creepy stories to fall asleep to
